ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general scallop food product class (FOODON_03302051) but no specific entry for Thai-style marinated tossed scallops. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers scallops as a seafood category. No specific code exists for Thai-marinated tossed scallop preparations. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144002 identifies scallop as a substance. No specific code for Thai-style marinated scallop dish. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical restaurant serving of tossed Thai style marinated scallops is approximately 200 g, comprising 4–6 large scallops with marinade sauce and garnish.

nutrient_source.title: Scallops, mixed species, raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 175180) adapted with Thai marinade sauce components estimated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base scallop values taken from USDA SR Legacy entry for raw mixed-species scallops (FDC ID 175180: energy 69 kcal, protein 12.1 g, fat 0.49 g, carbohydrate 3.18 g per 100 g). Thai marinade components (fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, chilli, garlic, sesame oil) contribute additional sodium, sugar, and minor fat. Composite per-100g values estimated by weighting scallop base (~75% by mass) against marinade contribution (~25% by mass). Marinade sodium estimated from USDA fish sauce entry (FDC ID 173209) and lime juice (FDC ID 167747). Final composite: energy ~88 kcal, protein ~9.1 g, fat ~1.8 g, carbohydrate ~7.2 g per 100 g. Dietary fibre estimated at 0.3 g per 100 g from vegetable garnish components. Sodium elevated due to fish sauce.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — shellfish and seafood dishes; analogous estimate based on mixed seafood with sauce (GI ~35)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai-style marinated scallops. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous seafood dishes with light sweet-sour sauce from the University of Sydney GI Database. Scallops are a high-protein, low-carbohydrate food; the marinade contributes sugar and lime juice which modestly raise GI above pure protein foods. GL per 200 g serving = (35 × 7.2 × 2.0) / 100 = 5.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce marinade (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1040 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish with caution due to the high sodium load from fish sauce; requesting reduced sauce or a smaller portion is advisable to keep sodium intake within the recommended 2000 mg/day limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~7.2 g per 100 g) with high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI and high protein content of scallops make this dish a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, though the added sugar in the Thai marinade should be factored into total carbohydrate budgeting for the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, high lean protein, and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from scallops
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Scallops are a lean, low-saturated-fat seafood with favourable omega-3 content; however, the elevated sodium from fish sauce warrants portion control for individuals managing cardiovascular disease and hypertension concurrently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tossed Thai Style Marinated Scallops (泰式捞带子) is a Chinese-Thai fusion seafood dish popular in Singapore's seafood restaurant scene. Fresh scallops are marinated in a vibrant Thai-inspired sauce comprising fish sauce, lime juice, fresh chilli, garlic, lemongrass, coriander, and a touch of sugar, then tossed to coat evenly before serving cold or at room temperature. The dish delivers a bright, tangy, mildly spicy flavour profile with the natural sweetness and tender, slightly chewy texture of the scallops complementing the aromatic marinade. Nutritionally, the dish is high in lean protein (~9 g per 100 g), low in saturated fat (~0.3 g per 100 g), and provides beneficial minerals including phosphorus, magnesium, selenium, and zinc from the scallops. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management. The primary nutritional concern is sodium, which is elevated (~520 mg per 100 g) due to the fish sauce base of the marinade. Clinically, this dish is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease in moderate portions, but those with hypertension should monitor sodium intake carefully. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free, and is commonly served as a starter or shared plate in Chinese seafood restaurants and hot pot establishments in Singapore.
common_user_queries: What are Thai style marinated scallops?
Is this dish suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of Thai scallop dish
Is Thai marinated scallop gluten free?
What is lo scallop in Chinese restaurant?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai-style marinated scallops exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy raw scallop data (FDC ID 175180) with marinade component estimation using additional USDA FoodData Central entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates: scallop base from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 175180) weighted ~75% by mass, Thai marinade components estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for fish sauce (FDC ID 173209), lime juice (FDC ID 167747), sugar, garlic, and sesame oil. 2. Sodium is elevated (~520 mg/100 g) primarily due to fish sauce in the marinade; actual restaurant sodium may vary significantly. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the source database. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible for this seafood-based dish with no hydrogenated fats. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per 200 g serving = (35 × 7.2 × 2.0) / 100 = 5.0. 6. Halal status is uncertain as fish sauce brands vary; marked is_halal false by default. 7. This dish is served at Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall, Singapore (GrabFood Singapore source). 8. The food_name in the input JSON (泰式捞带子 Tossed Thai Style Marinated Scallops) has been split: Chinese form preserved in local_names.chinese_simplified; canonical English name set as food_name.
